items.question: What does SEO primarily aim to improve?
items.correctAnswer: Visibility in unpaid (organic) search results for relevant queries.
incorrectAnswers: Only the cost of paid ads with no impact on organic results
The shipping time of products to customers
The legal compliance status of a website
items.explanation: SEO focuses on ranking and discoverability in search without paying directly for each click like ads.

items.question: What does search intent describe?
items.correctAnswer: What a searcher is trying to accomplish when they type a query.
incorrectAnswers: The weather conditions at the user’s location
The exact character limit for a page title
The company’s internal goal, unrelated to what the user wants
items.explanation: Search intent helps teams align content with why users searched—informational, navigational, or transactional.

items.question: What is a SERP snippet?
items.correctAnswer: The short preview of information shown in search results under a link.
incorrectAnswers: The company’s shipping label on an order
A legally required audit form for search engines
The internal list of all keywords never shown to users
items.explanation: Snippets are what searchers see—title and summary text can influence click-through behavior.

items.question: What is typically an example of on-page SEO?
items.correctAnswer: Optimizing page content and elements like headings to better match search intent.
incorrectAnswers: Paying for a sponsored placement in search results
Only requesting back-end server upgrades with no content changes
Eliminating all internal links so crawlers can’t navigate
items.explanation: On-page SEO covers what’s on the page—content quality, structure, and relevance—so search engines can understand it.

items.question: What is “technical SEO” mainly about?
items.correctAnswer: Making a site crawlable and understandable so search engines can access and interpret pages.
incorrectAnswers: Only writing blog posts, with no regard for site structure
Avoiding any metadata so pages are always hidden
Ensuring every page has the same word count
items.explanation: Technical SEO covers site performance and structure elements that affect crawling, indexing, and rendering.

items.question: Why are backlinks often discussed in SEO?
items.correctAnswer: They can help signal authority and relevance to search engines.
incorrectAnswers: They are required to submit taxes correctly
They only affect the website’s colors and branding
They guarantee ranking positions regardless of content
items.explanation: Backlinks act as endorsements from other sites, influencing how search engines evaluate authority and credibility.
